title: Determination of total water-soluble phosphorus in solid agricultural samples using the ascorbic acid colorimetric method
author: Cestari-Abreu, Sebastián
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The purpose of the seminar is to extend a theoretical training on the determination of total water-soluble phosphorus of solid agricultural samples by means of the ascorbic acid colorimetric method. The presentation begins with an introduction, in which the different forms of phosphorus in the soil are discerned; special attention receives the concept of bioavailable phosphorus due to its practical implications. The course provides guidance for the appropriate preparation of samples and for the subsequent extraction of water-soluble phosphorus; emphasis is placed on the arguments for using water as an extraction agent for compost, and on the following need to digest the extract to obtain the total soluble phosphorus. The seminar continues with an explanation of how the ascorbic acid method works; the material extends rigorous training in the mathematical calculations involved, in particular, in those related in the adjustment of the calibration curve to the potential mass of phosphorus in the aqueous extract. The presentation concludes with a brief description of the various extraction agents for measuring bioavailable phosphorus in soil samples, as well as the parameters that these soils must meet to undertake each type of extraction; Olsen extraction receives special attention due to its popularity among Venezuelan laboratories.
